sections.content: SPACE changed the board on odd-numbered presses (1, 4, 7, 10). This is a pattern! It seems like SPACE alternates between changing and not changing. Let me check what changed.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white-green block is still at rows 44-47, cols 28-31. The pink HUD bar changed from 59 to 55 pixels, and the charcoal at the bottom changed from 5 to 9 pixels. So the white-green block didn't change.
Wait, the white-green block is still at the same position. The pink bar changed. So SPACE is not moving the white-green block. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, looking at the pattern: SPACE changed the board on presses 1, 4, 7, 10. This is every 3rd press. So the pattern is: change, no change, no change, change, change, no change, no change, change, no change, no change, change, no change.
This is very strange. Let me try a different approach. Let me try pressing SPACE while moving the cursor in different directions to see if the cursor position matters.
Actually, let me re-examine the first few actions. When I pressed SPACE initially, SPACE didn't change anything. Then I pressed LEFT, and SPACE changed the board (white-green moved left). Then I pressed UP, and SPACE changed (white-green moved up). Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE changed (white-green merged). Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE changed (unmerged). Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE didn't change. Then I pressed SPACE changed (merged).
So the pattern seems to be: SPACE alternates between merging and unmerging the white-green block and unmerging them. The white-green block is moving around the board.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me try to understand what the cursor is. Maybe the cursor is the white-green block itself, and pressing SPACE moves the cursor to the next object in some order.
Actually, I think I think the key insight is that SPACE is not moving the cursor, but rather SPACE is toggling between merged/unmerged state of the white-green block. The block is moving because the merge/unmerge action causes the block to shift position.
Let me try to understand the goal. Maybe I need to merge all objects together. Let me try to move the white-green block to the center and press SPACE repeatedly to see if it merges with other objects.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try to move the white-green block to the center and press SPACE
# First, let me try moving RIGHT to see if the block moves right.
result = action(['RIGHT')
print("RIGHT:", result)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
